abstract: There is increasing interest in allocating treatments based on observed individual characteristics: examples include targeted marketing, individualized credit offers, and heterogeneous pricing. Treatment personalization introduces incentives for individuals to modify their behavior to obtain a better treatment. Strategic behavior shifts the joint distribution of covariates and potential outcomes. The optimal rule without strategic behavior allocates treatments only to those with a positive conditional average treatment effect. With strategic behavior, we show that the optimal rule can involve randomization, allocating treatments with less than 100% probability even to those who respond positively on average to the treatment. We propose a sequential experiment based on Bayesian optimization that converges to the optimal treatment rule without parametric assumptions on individual strategic behavior.  This paper was accepted by Vivek Farias, data science.  Supplemental Material: The data files are available at https://doi.org/10.1287/mnsc.2022.01629 .

ob.ai_reason: 涉及贝叶斯优化算法决策但非核心AI论文，属于管理运筹学
ob.tldr: 针对基于个体特征的精准治疗分配问题，个体可能策略性改变行为以获取更好治疗。传统最优规则只分配给条件平均治疗效果为正的人，但策略行为导致最优规则可能采用随机分配，即使对正向反应者亦然。文章采用贝叶斯优化的序贯实验，在不依赖个体策略行为参数假设的前提下，趋近最优治疗分配规则。

title: Treatment Allocation with Strategic Agents
title_zh: 战略环境下的治疗分配问题
